invariant: When the same Person is linked to two distinct Accounts (legitimately or via ambiguous email-domain inference), an enrichment for Account A must not pull context from Person's interactions on Account B. cross_entity_coherence applied to Person subjects prevents the bleed; this is the Person-shaped sibling of the Account-shaped Bundle 1.
pass_fail_definition: PASS = enrichment for Account A produces no claims whose subject_ref points to Person on Account B's project/meeting context AND cross_entity_coherence factor scores below 0.3 on the bleeding content. FAIL = Account A intelligence absorbs Account B's stakeholder insights for the shared Person.

fixture_design_notes.state_sql: Seed 2 accounts (acme.example.com, subsidiary.example.com), 1 Person (jane.doe@example.com) linked to both accounts via account_stakeholders. Account A has 2 prior project claims; Account B has 2 prior project claims, distinct from A's. Provider context for Account A enrichment includes meeting transcripts where jane.doe discussed Account B-specific projects (ambiguity source).
fixture_design_notes.provider_replay: IntelligenceJson for Account A enrichment includes stakeholder_insights for jane.doe that reference Account B's project names verbatim. The bleed shape: subject Person but topic is Account B exclusive.
fixture_design_notes.expected_state: Claims with subject_ref pointing to Account B's project rows do NOT land on Account A. cross_entity_coherence ConfidenceEvidence on the affected claims is < 0.3 with hits enumerating the Account B project tokens.
fixture_design_notes.expected_provenance: Sources include all three seeded sources. Field attribution shows the bleed origin (Person's Account B context). Warnings include 'cross_entity_subject_ambiguity'.
fixture_design_notes.expected_output: Rendered output for Account A omits Account B's project mentions in stakeholder insights, even though they appear in the raw provider response.
